Title: Strigolactones enhance root-knot nematode (Meloidogyne graminicola) infection in rice by antagonizing the jasmonate pathway

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling and plant hormone controlling development; in this paper, promotes susceptibility to root-knot nematode infection by antagonizing jasmonate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript in roots; induced trend at 1 dpi after Meloidogyne graminicola infection but not significantly different from control
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; measured at early infection stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Meloidogyne graminicola infection; GR24 application; TIS108 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D27 function reduces nematode infection and increases jasmonate accumulation; exogenous GR24 restores susceptibility in the biosynthetic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The d27 strigolactone-biosynthesis mutant showed reduced total nematode number and reduced nematode development compared with wild type. 2. d27 roots accumulated higher cis-OPDA, JA, and JA-Ile than wild type. 3. Low-dose GR24 restored susceptibility in d27, supporting a causal role of strigolactone biosynthesis in promoting infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (d27); foliar GR24 complementation; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; qRT-PCR pathway expression; nematode infection phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf phenotype and altered root architecture reported for d mutants; however reduced susceptibility was not explained by fewer root tips in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing D27 function or strigolactone biosynthesis may improve resistance to root-knot nematode in rice, but likely with architectural/yield penalties associated with dwarf strigolactone mutants; partial modulation rather than full knockout may be preferable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses d27 primarily as a biosynthetic mutant; direct enzymatic assay was not performed here, but assignment is standard for rice SL biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling and developmental hormone; associated here with promotion of nematode susceptibility through JA antagonism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly induced in roots at 1 dpi after Meloidogyne graminicola infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with infection-induced activation of the strigolactone biosynthetic pathway in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Osd17 transcript levels were significantly induced in nematode-infected roots at 1 dpi. 2. This induction supports activation of the strigolactone pathway during early infection. 3. Functional genetics in this paper focused mainly on d10 and d27 biosynthetic mutants and d14 signaling mutant rather than d17 mutant lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target to reduce SL-mediated susceptibility, but not directly validated genetically in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because it was experimentally assayed in Results as part of the induced SL biosynthetic pathway, although no d17 mutant phenotype was tested in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere and hormonal signaling; in this paper, promotes susceptibility to root-knot nematode by suppressing jasmonate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly induced in roots at 1 dpi after Meloidogyne graminicola infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D10 function reduces nematode infection and elevates jasmonates; GR24 restores susceptibility and lowers jasmonates in the biosynthetic mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Osd10 was transcriptionally induced by nematode infection at 1 dpi. 2. The d10 biosynthetic mutant had fewer nematodes and egg-laying females than wild type. 3. d10 roots accumulated more cis-OPDA, JA, and JA-Ile. 4. Low-dose GR24 restored susceptibility and reduced jasmonate levels in d10.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (d10); foliar GR24 complementation; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; qRT-PCR; nematode infection phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarfism and altered root system traits typical of SL-deficient mutants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: D10 is a promising target to reduce SL-mediated susceptibility to root-knot nematode, but complete loss likely causes architectural penalties; tunable editing may be more practical
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper functionally supports D10 as a positive contributor to susceptibility via JA antagonism rather than by directly measuring SL content.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal and rhizosphere signal; here acts to promote nematode susceptibility by suppressing jasmonate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript significantly induced in roots at 1 dpi after Meloidogyne graminicola infection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Meloidogyne graminicola infection; exogenous GR24; endogenous strigolactones
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Jasmonate accumulation/JA pathway (functional antagonism demonstrated); exact direct downstream target genes not defined
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses jasmonate accumulation/signaling output in roots; direct target genes not identified
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Hormone signaling crosstalk controlling jasmonate-mediated defense and susceptibility to nematode infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Hormone perception/signaling leading to negative regulation of jasmonate pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential SL signaling component required for SL/GR24-dependent enhancement of nematode susceptibility and repression of jasmonate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of D14 signaling reduces nematode infection and elevates JA and JA-Ile; GR24 cannot restore susceptibility or suppress jasmonates in d14
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Osd14 expression was induced by nematode infection. 2. The d14 signaling mutant was less susceptible to M. graminicola and accumulated more jasmonates than wild type. 3. Low-dose GR24 failed to restore susceptibility or reduce jasmonates in d14, showing that D14-mediated signaling is necessary for the effect.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (d14); GR24 non-complementation; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; qRT-PCR; nematode infection phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf phenotype and altered root architecture associated with disrupted strigolactone signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Disrupting or attenuating D14 signaling could enhance nematode resistance, but developmental penalties from impaired SL signaling must be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although D14 is a receptor rather than transcription factor, it is a core regulatory gene in this study because signaling competence was essential for the metabolite-defense phenotype.
